title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a local theatre production they just saw.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: That was quite an intense performance of 'Macbeth'. I’m still a bit shaken! || Man: [1]I found the modern setting with the business suits and smartphones a bit distracting[/1], to be honest. It didn't really mesh with the dialogue for me. || Woman: I see what you mean. It was a bold choice. || Man: [2]What I really admired, though, was the way they used the lighting to create a sense of unease[/2]. Those long shadows on the stage were very effective. || Woman: I agree. It was atmospheric without being over-the-top. || Man: Exactly. It’s just a shame the lead actor’s voice was a bit thin. He got drowned out by the sound effects a few times.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That was quite an intense performance of 'Macbeth'. I’m still a bit shaken!

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I found the modern setting with the business suits and smartphones a bit distracting, to be honest. It didn't really mesh with the dialogue for me.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It was a bold choice.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What I really admired, though, was the way they used the lighting to create a sense of unease. Those long shadows on the stage were very effective.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. It was atmospheric without being over-the-top.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s just a shame the lead actor’s voice was a bit thin. He got drowned out by the sound effects a few times.

questions.question: The man disliked the play because

options.B: the contemporary updates felt out of place.
options.C: the sound quality was consistently poor.

questions.explanation: He found the 'modern setting' (suits and smartphones) distracting and felt it didn't 'mesh with the dialogue' [1].

questions.question: What aspect of the production did the man find successful?

options.B: the visual mood created by the lighting
options.C: the emotional range of the lead performer

questions.explanation: He admired how the lighting created a 'sense of unease' through 'long shadows' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues discussing a new 'unlimited holiday' policy at their firm.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, the new holiday policy is live. It’s pretty revolutionary for a law firm, isn't it? || Woman: [3]I suspect people will actually end up taking less time off because they’re worried about appearing less committed than their peers[/3]. || Man: That's a classic psychological trap. I’m already feeling guilty about my week in Spain. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that management hasn't provided any clear guidelines on how to handle urgent client work during absences[/4]. It’s all a bit vague. || Man: I agree. It’s fine in theory, but without a proper system for coverage, it’s just going to lead to more stress. || Woman: Exactly. It sounds great in a recruitment brochure, but the reality is much more complicated.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, the new holiday policy is live. It’s pretty revolutionary for a law firm, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect people will actually end up taking less time off because they’re worried about appearing less committed than their peers.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a classic psychological trap. I’m already feeling guilty about my week in Spain.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that management hasn't provided any clear guidelines on how to handle urgent client work during absences. It’s all a bit vague.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s fine in theory, but without a proper system for coverage, it’s just going to lead to more stress.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It sounds great in a recruitment brochure, but the reality is much more complicated.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy might lead to

options.A: a decrease in the quality of client service.
options.B: employees feeling pressured to avoid taking leave.
options.C: a significant increase in administrative costs.

questions.explanation: She thinks people will take less time off because they don't want to appear 'less committed' [3].

questions.question: What is the speakers' main concern regarding the policy?

options.A: the potential for staff to abuse the system
options.B: the lack of a practical framework for implementation
options.C: the negative impact on the company's reputation

questions.explanation: They both agree that the lack of 'clear guidelines' and a 'system for coverage' makes it problematic [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently published biography of a famous scientist.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve just finished that new book on Marie Curie. It’s a real page-turner for a biography. || Woman: [5]I found the author's habit of imagining the subject's private thoughts a bit off-putting[/5]. It felt more like historical fiction at times. || Man: I see your point. It did take some liberties with the internal dialogue. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the way it contextualized her work within the social limitations of the time[/6], though. It really highlighted how much she had to overcome. || Man: I agree. The research into the academic politics of 19th-century Paris was top-notch. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a very thorough piece of historical research, even if the narrative style is a bit unconventional.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve just finished that new book on Marie Curie. It’s a real page-turner for a biography.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the author's habit of imagining the subject's private thoughts a bit off-putting. It felt more like historical fiction at times.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. It did take some liberties with the internal dialogue.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the way it contextualized her work within the social limitations of the time, though. It really highlighted how much she had to overcome.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. The research into the academic politics of 19th-century Paris was top-notch.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a very thorough piece of historical research, even if the narrative style is a bit unconventional.

questions.question: What was the woman's main criticism of the biography?

options.A: the lack of focus on scientific achievements
options.B: the inclusion of speculative personal details
options.C: the poor quality of the primary sources

questions.explanation: She disliked the author 'imagining the subject's private thoughts' [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the author's
questions.explanation: They both highlight the research into 'social limitations' and 'academic politics' as being top-notch [6].
